bead.title: no-auth-namespace: --no-auth per-URL in-memory namespace
bead.description:
--no-auth mode per-URL in-memory namespace. When the server runs with --no-auth, every request synthesizes an anonymous admin claim, and the storage materializes a per-URL (tenant, database) namespace on first touch. No persistent bootstrap, no user rows, no tenant rows written to disk.

## Scope

### 1. No-auth synthetic claim helper

Add to crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs (or a new module crates/axon-server/src/no_auth.rs):

    /// Synthesize a ResolvedIdentity for --no-auth mode.
    ///
    /// The claim carries the anonymous user UUID (a deterministic zero UUID),
    /// the tenant and database names extracted from the URL path, and an
    /// admin grant on the database.
    pub fn synthesize_no_auth_identity(
        tenant: &str,
        database: &str,
    ) -> ResolvedIdentity {
        ResolvedIdentity {
            user_id: UserId::nil(),  // deterministic anonymous UUID
            tenant_id: TenantId::from_name(tenant),  // hashable mapping
            grants: Grants {
                databases: vec![GrantedDatabase {
                    name: database.to_string(),
                    ops: vec![Op::Read, Op::Write, Op::Admin],
                }],
            },
        }
    }

TenantId::from_name is a deterministic hash-to-UUID (use uuid::Uuid::new_v5 with a fixed namespace). This keeps the ID stable across calls for the same name without needing a DB lookup.

### 2. Middleware/layer glue

Add a new axum middleware in crates/axon-server/src/no_auth.rs (NEW):

    pub async fn no_auth_layer(
        resolved_path: Option<Extension<ResolvedPath>>,
        mut req: Request,
        next: Next,
    ) -> Response {
        if let Some(Extension(path)) = resolved_path {
            let identity = synthesize_no_auth_identity(&path.tenant, &path.database);
            req.extensions_mut().insert(identity);
        }
        next.run(req).await
    }

Uses D1's ResolvedPath extension as the upstream input. This means the path_router_layer from D1 must run BEFORE no_auth_layer — add a doc comment saying so.

### 3. In-memory namespace behavior

The storage adapter layer doesn't need to change — the existing memory adapter already materializes (tenant, database) key spaces on demand. What this bead adds is the PROOF that --no-auth reaches the right adapter + namespace.

Integration test proves:
- Writing to /tenants/a/databases/x/... and reading back succeeds (same namespace)
- Writing to /tenants/a/databases/x/... and reading /tenants/b/databases/y/... returns 404 (different namespace)
- After server restart (on an in-memory adapter), everything is gone

### 4. Tests: crates/axon-server/tests/no_auth_test.rs (NEW)

#### synthesize_returns_admin_grant
Unit: call synthesize_no_auth_identity("acme", "orders") and assert grants = [{ name: "orders", ops: [read, write, admin] }].

#### tenant_id_is_deterministic
Unit: two calls with the same (tenant, database) return the same tenant_id.

#### different_names_different_ids
Unit: ("a", "x") and ("b", "x") produce different tenant_ids.

#### middleware_installs_identity
Integration: axum router with path_router_layer + no_auth_layer. Hit /tenants/acme/databases/orders/ping and assert the handler sees ResolvedIdentity with admin grants on "orders".

#### middleware_skips_when_path_not_data_plane
Hit /health — no ResolvedPath extracted, no ResolvedIdentity installed, handler sees no identity.

## Out of scope

- Wiring no_auth_layer into the real router at serve.rs — that's D3's job.
- The storage adapter per-URL namespace materialization — already works for the memory adapter.
- --no-auth + Tailscale fallback — B3 federation handles the Tailscale path; --no-auth is the "no auth at all" path.

## Context

- D1 landed path_router_layer + ResolvedPath
- B1 landed ResolvedIdentity (in axon-core)
- A2: Grants, GrantedDatabase, Op, TenantId, UserId

## Files to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/no_auth.rs — NEW (both synthesize fn + layer)
- crates/axon-server/src/lib.rs — pub mod no_auth; re-export
- crates/axon-core/src/auth.rs — add TenantId::from_name helper (uses uuid::Uuid::new_v5 with a fixed namespace)
- crates/axon-server/tests/no_auth_test.rs — NEW

## Files NOT to touch

- crates/axon-server/src/serve.rs — router wiring is D3
- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale
- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, no_auth is a peer module

## Verification

- cargo test -p axon-server --test no_auth_test (5 tests pass)
- cargo test -p axon-core (TenantId::from_name unit test)
- cargo test --workspace (all prior green)
- cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
bead.acceptance:
- [ ] crates/axon-server/src/no_auth.rs with synthesize_no_auth_identity + no_auth_layer
- [ ] TenantId::from_name helper in axon-core
- [ ] no_auth_test.rs with 5 tests (unit + middleware integration)
- [ ] Deterministic tenant_id mapping
- [ ] Admin grants on the URL database
- [ ] cargo test --workspace green
- [ ] cargo clippy --workspace -- -D warnings clean
